clusters.title: Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire as stalemate continues in peace talks
clusters.neutral_headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
bullet_summary: Kuwait’s military said it was intercepting drones and missiles launched at the country
Bahrain, home to the US Navy's Fifth Fleet, denounced the strikes, while Kuwait said they "represent a dangerous escalation"

items.title: Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire as stalemate continues in peace talks
items.standfirst: The U.S. military reported that it shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz overnight Saturday local time, while seven ballistic missiles fired at Kuwait and Bahrain were largely intercepted as well.
items.content: The U.S. military reported that it shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz overnight Saturday local time, while seven ballistic missiles fired at Kuwait and Bahrain were largely intercepted as well.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
items.standfirst: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.
items.content: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

items.title: Kuwait and Bahrain targeted by Iran after exchange of fire with US
items.standfirst: Iran attacks American bases in Gulf states after Washington shoots down drones and strikes Iranian radar sitesBahrain has said Iran fired ballistic missiles and drones at it and Kuwait, hours after the US and Iran exchanged strikes over the Gulf, the latest in a series of flare-ups that threatened to break the fragile ceasefire.Air raid sirens rang out on Saturday in Bahrain and people were told to move to a safe location and await further instructions. Kuwait’s military said it was intercepting drones and missiles launched at the country. Continue reading...
items.content: Iran attacks American bases in Gulf states after Washington shoots down drones and strikes Iranian radar sitesBahrain has said Iran fired ballistic missiles and drones at it and Kuwait, hours after the US and Iran exchanged strikes over the Gulf, the latest in a series of flare-ups that threatened to break the fragile ceasefire.Air raid sirens rang out on Saturday in Bahrain and people were told to move to a safe location and await further instructions. Kuwait’s military said it was intercepting drones and missiles launched at the country. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran launches ballistic missiles at Bahrain, Kuwait after US hits its radar sites
items.standfirst: Iran on Saturday fired ballistic missiles and drones at US allies Bahrain and Kuwait hours after the US conducted strikes on Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in tit-for-tat attacks that further strained the fragile ceasefire. Bahrain, home to the US Navy's Fifth Fleet, denounced the strikes, while Kuwait said they "represent a dangerous escalation".
items.content: Iran on Saturday fired ballistic missiles and drones at US allies Bahrain and Kuwait hours after the US conducted strikes on Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in tit-for-tat attacks that further strained the fragile ceasefire. Bahrain, home to the US Navy's Fifth Fleet, denounced the strikes, while Kuwait said they "represent a dangerous escalation".

clusters.title: Palestinian baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
clusters.neutral_headline: Palestinian baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
bullet_summary: A seven-month-old baby has been killed and his parents were wounded in the West Bank after Israeli soldiers opened fire, the Palestinian health ministry said
Reported by Guardian World, BBC World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Palestinian baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
items.standfirst: The seven-month-old, Sam Fahd Abu Haikal, was in his mother’s arms when soldiers fired on family in HebronIsraeli troops killed a seven-month-old Palestinian baby in the occupied West Bank and injured his parents after opening fire on the family’s car, despite it having complied with an order to stop.Soldiers opened fire on Friday on a car carrying the infant and his parents in the Tel Rumeida area of Hebron. The seven-month-old, Sam Fahd Abu Haikal, was critically injured, evacuated in critical condition to a hospital, where he later died. Continue reading...
items.content: The seven-month-old, Sam Fahd Abu Haikal, was in his mother’s arms when soldiers fired on family in HebronIsraeli troops killed a seven-month-old Palestinian baby in the occupied West Bank and injured his parents after opening fire on the family’s car, despite it having complied with an order to stop.Soldiers opened fire on Friday on a car carrying the infant and his parents in the Tel Rumeida area of Hebron. The seven-month-old, Sam Fahd Abu Haikal, was critically injured, evacuated in critical condition to a hospital, where he later died. Continue reading...

items.title: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
items.standfirst: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.
items.content: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

items.title: Palestinian father buries baby killed by Israeli gunfire in West Bank
items.standfirst: A Palestinian father in Hebron has buried his seven-month-old son after the baby was killed by Israeli gunfire
items.content: A Palestinian father in Hebron has buried his seven-month-old son after the baby was killed by Israeli gunfire

clusters.title: Russian teenager Mirra Andreeva wins French Open to claim first Grand Slam
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian teenager Mirra Andreeva wins French Open to claim first Grand Slam
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Russian teenager Mirra Andreeva wins French Open to claim first Grand Slam
items.standfirst: Eighth-ranked Mirra Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in 6-3, 6-2 win in final.
items.content: Eighth-ranked Mirra Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in 6-3, 6-2 win in final.

items.title: Russian teen claims first Grand Slam tennis title with French Open win
items.standfirst: The eighth-ranked Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in the French Open final on Saturday.
items.content: The eighth-ranked Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in the French Open final on Saturday.

items.title: Andreeva wins French Open as Chwalinska fairytale ends
items.standfirst: Teenager Mirra Andreeva fulfils her huge potential with a first Grand Slam title as Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska is denied a fairytale French Open victory.
items.content: Teenager Mirra Andreeva fulfils her huge potential with a first Grand Slam title as Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska is denied a fairytale French Open victory.

items.title: Russian Andreeva beats Poland's Chwalinska to win French Open title
items.standfirst: Russian ​eighth seed ​Mirra Andreeva beat Polish qualifier ​Maja ‌Chwalinska ⁠6-3 ‌6-2 to win the ⁠French Open and capture ​her maiden ‌Grand Slam title on ‌Saturday. The 19-year-old ​became the first teenager to win ​the ​Roland Garros ​crown since Poland's Iga Swiatek in 2020.
items.content: Russian ​eighth seed ​Mirra Andreeva beat Polish qualifier ​Maja ‌Chwalinska ⁠6-3 ‌6-2 to win the ⁠French Open and capture ​her maiden ‌Grand Slam title on ‌Saturday. The 19-year-old ​became the first teenager to win ​the ​Roland Garros ​crown since Poland's Iga Swiatek in 2020.

items.title: French Open women's final set to make history as Chwalinska faces Andreeva
items.standfirst: The French Open women's final is guaranteed to make history, with Poland's Maja Chwalinska aiming to become the first qualifier to win the title and Russia's Mirra Andreeva seeking to become the youngest champion since 1992. The match takes place on Saturday at Roland Garros. FRANCE 24's Jamaes Vasina reports.
items.content: The French Open women's final is guaranteed to make history, with Poland's Maja Chwalinska aiming to become the first qualifier to win the title and Russia's Mirra Andreeva seeking to become the youngest champion since 1992. The match takes place on Saturday at Roland Garros. FRANCE 24's Jamaes Vasina reports.

clusters.title: Ukrainian drones target St Petersburg in attack Russia calls 'unprecedented'

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NBC World, France24

items.title: Ukrainian drones target St Petersburg in attack Russia calls 'unprecedented'
items.standfirst: Residents of Russia's second-largest city were urged to stay indoors for the first time since the war started.
items.content: Residents of Russia's second-largest city were urged to stay indoors for the first time since the war started.

items.title: Russia opens door to ‘new relationship’ with U.S. as Putin wrestles with Ukraine deadlock
items.standfirst: ST. PETERSBURG, Russia — President Vladimir Putin looked out across a familiar scene on Friday — and a changing world
items.content: ST. PETERSBURG, Russia — President Vladimir Putin looked out across a familiar scene on Friday — and a changing world

items.title: Ukraine launches fresh drone attack on St. Petersburg region on final day of ‘Russian Davos’
items.standfirst: Ukraine on Saturday fired hundreds of drones targeting the St. Petersburg region in the second such attack on Russia’s second-largest city in less than a week. The attack came on the final day of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, President Vladimir Putin’s annual investment forum known as “Russia’s Davos”.
items.content: Ukraine on Saturday fired hundreds of drones targeting the St. Petersburg region in the second such attack on Russia’s second-largest city in less than a week. The attack came on the final day of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, President Vladimir Putin’s annual investment forum known as “Russia’s Davos”.

items.title: Putin rules out Zelenskyy meeting, touts a new world order at ‘Russian Davos’
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin declined a request to meet Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskky in a speech at a flagship annual economic forum in St. Petersburg.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin declined a request to meet Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskky in a speech at a flagship annual economic forum in St. Petersburg.

clusters.title: Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort stokes days of protests in Albania
clusters.neutral_headline: Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort stokes days of protests in Albania
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, NBC World

items.title: Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort stokes days of protests in Albania
items.standfirst: The so-called "Flamingo Revolution" has taken up the cause of protecting the Albanian coast from a development led by the president's son-in-law.
items.content: The so-called "Flamingo Revolution" has taken up the cause of protecting the Albanian coast from a development led by the president's son-in-law.

items.title: Albanians protest plans for Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort on the country's coast
items.standfirst: Crowds of Albanians gathered in Tirana to continue protesting plans for a resort backed by President Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner, set to go up on the country's Adriatic coast. CBS News' Emmet Lyons reports.
items.content: Crowds of Albanians gathered in Tirana to continue protesting plans for a resort backed by President Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner, set to go up on the country's Adriatic coast. CBS News' Emmet Lyons reports.

items.title: Albanians protest Jared Kushner-backed resort plan on nature reserve
items.standfirst: Protesters on Saturday gathered at the Vjosa-Narta lagoon, a nature reserve on the Albanian coast, to denounce a plan by US President Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner, to build a ​luxury resort inan ‌environmentally sensitive area. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama has insisted that "top" experts will be involved in the project, which has yet to be approved.
items.content: Protesters on Saturday gathered at the Vjosa-Narta lagoon, a nature reserve on the Albanian coast, to denounce a plan by US President Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner, to build a ​luxury resort inan ‌environmentally sensitive area. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama has insisted that "top" experts will be involved in the project, which has yet to be approved.

items.title: Backlash in Albania over plans for new luxury resort backed by Kushner
items.standfirst: A new luxury development in Albania, backed by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, is drawing backlash from locals citing environmental concerns. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.
items.content: A new luxury development in Albania, backed by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, is drawing backlash from locals citing environmental concerns. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

clusters.title: Bernadette Chirac, formidable former first lady of France, dies aged 93
clusters.neutral_headline: Bernadette Chirac, formidable former first lady of France, dies aged 93

items.title: Bernadette Chirac, formidable former first lady of France, dies aged 93
items.standfirst: Widow of French ex-president Jacques Chirac was a steely behind-the-scenes operator known for her charity workBernadette Chirac, the formidable widow of the former French president Jacques Chirac and a driving force behind his political rise, has died at the age of 93.As France’s first lady for 12 years, Chirac was a steely behind-the-scenes operator in support of her husband, who served twice as prime minister, 18 years as mayor of Paris and two terms as president. Continue reading...
items.content: Widow of French ex-president Jacques Chirac was a steely behind-the-scenes operator known for her charity workBernadette Chirac, the formidable widow of the former French president Jacques Chirac and a driving force behind his political rise, has died at the age of 93.As France’s first lady for 12 years, Chirac was a steely behind-the-scenes operator in support of her husband, who served twice as prime minister, 18 years as mayor of Paris and two terms as president. Continue reading...

items.title: A look back at French former first lady Bernadette Chirac's legacy
items.standfirst: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside President Jacques Chirac – weathering his notorious infidelities with dry humor while building her own political power base in rural France – has died. She was 93. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks down her legacy.
items.content: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside President Jacques Chirac – weathering his notorious infidelities with dry humor while building her own political power base in rural France – has died. She was 93. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks down her legacy.

items.title: Bernadette Chirac, former first lady of France, dies aged 93
items.standfirst: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside President Jacques Chirac while building her own political power in rural Corrèze and turning a children’s hospital charity into a national institution, has died. She was 93.
items.content: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside President Jacques Chirac while building her own political power in rural Corrèze and turning a children’s hospital charity into a national institution, has died. She was 93.

items.title: Former French first lady dies at 93: Bernadette Chirac, a 'discreet force'
items.standfirst: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside president Jacques Chirac while building her own political power in rural Corrèze and turning a children’s hospital charity into a national institution, has died. She was 93. Beloved by many, Bernadette was a "discreet force" and the "rock" on which her husband's political career was based on, according to Paul Smith, head of modern languages and associate professor in French at the University of Nottingham.
items.content: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside president Jacques Chirac while building her own political power in rural Corrèze and turning a children’s hospital charity into a national institution, has died. She was 93. Beloved by many, Bernadette was a "discreet force" and the "rock" on which her husband's political career was based on, according to Paul Smith, head of modern languages and associate professor in French at the University of Nottingham.

items.title: Bernadette Chirac, widow of former French president, dies at 93
items.standfirst: Bernadette Chirac, the widow of former French president Jacques Chirac and a fixed point in her late husband's climb to the country's top political post, died on Saturday. She was 93.
items.content: Bernadette Chirac, the widow of former French president Jacques Chirac and a fixed point in her late husband's climb to the country's top political post, died on Saturday. She was 93.

clusters.title: David Sullivan steps down at West Ham to fight claims about private life
clusters.neutral_headline: David Sullivan steps down at West Ham to fight claims about private life
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: David Sullivan steps down at West Ham to fight claims about private life
items.standfirst: Joint-chair of relegated club to tackle ‘false allegations’‘I am not the person the media has decided to paint me as’The former pornography baron David Sullivan has announced his resignation as a joint-chair and director of West Ham with immediate effect.Sullivan and his legal representatives said in a statement that the 77-year-old billionaire was stepping down to apply his “full energy and attention” to fighting what he described as “false allegations” concerning his personal conduct, due to be aired as part of a joint investigation by BBC Panorama and the Times on Monday. Continue reading...
items.content: Joint-chair of relegated club to tackle ‘false allegations’‘I am not the person the media has decided to paint me as’The former pornography baron David Sullivan has announced his resignation as a joint-chair and director of West Ham with immediate effect.Sullivan and his legal representatives said in a statement that the 77-year-old billionaire was stepping down to apply his “full energy and attention” to fighting what he described as “false allegations” concerning his personal conduct, due to be aired as part of a joint investigation by BBC Panorama and the Times on Monday. Continue reading...

items.title: West Ham joint chairman David Sullivan steps down after 'serious' historical allegations
items.standfirst: Sullivan is stepping down from his position as joint chairman and director of the club with immediate effect "having been made aware of the impending publication of serious historic allegations".
items.content: Sullivan is stepping down from his position as joint chairman and director of the club with immediate effect "having been made aware of the impending publication of serious historic allegations".

clusters.title: Parliamentary elections mark 'historical moment' for Armenia, expert says
clusters.neutral_headline: Armenia braces for election as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government

items.title: Parliamentary elections mark 'historical moment' for Armenia, expert says
items.standfirst: Armenia's upcoming parliamentary elections, which could reshape the country's ties with Russia and the West, mark a "historic moment" for the country, according to Ulrich Schmid, Professor of Eastern European Studies at the University of St Gallen. Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking to normalise relations with Turkey and neighbouring Azerbaijan, with which Armenia has been locked in conflict for decades.
items.content: Armenia's upcoming parliamentary elections, which could reshape the country's ties with Russia and the West, mark a "historic moment" for the country, according to Ulrich Schmid, Professor of Eastern European Studies at the University of St Gallen. Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking to normalise relations with Turkey and neighbouring Azerbaijan, with which Armenia has been locked in conflict for decades.

items.title: Armenia prepares for vote amid shifting relations with Russia and the West
items.standfirst: Armenia's parliamentary elections Sunday will be a vote on its geopolitical future as incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer relations with the European Union and the United States despite longstanding ties with Russia that have been championed by his critics. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports.
items.content: Armenia's parliamentary elections Sunday will be a vote on its geopolitical future as incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer relations with the European Union and the United States despite longstanding ties with Russia that have been championed by his critics. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports.

items.title: Armenia braces for election as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
items.standfirst: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.
items.content: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.
